artifacts.content:
From: Dalen Harrow <d.harrow@rustedcrown.local>
To: Elwen Tallowdraft <e.tallowdraft@scribesguild.grey>
Date: 12 Harvestwane 1848
Subject: Notes for your records – contacts & loose ends

Elwen,

You asked me to set down my current situation and useful names so you can keep your files straight. Here it is, as plain as I can manage.

First, on the “game of chances” you keep likening my life to: if you’re still tracking my aptitude the way you do with your numbers, remember how swingy my reflexes can be. Best to say I’m no better than a solid craftsman – not some flawless prodigy. Think of it as capping my “dexterity” at a respectable level, and only letting it climb if I actually earn it by surviving the fallout from my indefinite unpaid leave that started on 3rd Harvestmoon 1847, when Captain Roderic Vale pulled me off the South Gate roster over that botched alley arrest near the Old Tanner’s Guildhouse.

For your contact ledger, you listed me under “appropriate contacts > name[0] > type: Contact (City Watch Sergeant).” Leave the rank there if it helps you remember what I used to be, but note that the pay’s been cut off and the badge sits in a drawer.

Under cultural notes, you can mark me as Arvelian Kingdom, native Greyford lower-class, with extra familiarity in the Thornwood Borderlands from that 18‑month stint escorting the mixed caravans. You already know the rest – including Maera Thorn‑kin at Redfen Ford.

As for special complications: put Captain Mereth Vann of the Internal Discipline Board down as an active Enemy. He’s been after me since the warehouse raid on 12 Harvestfall 1847, when I ignored his order and broke Pell Dorn’s wrist in the lower cells. The summons from the Citadel still arrive every few months.

If you need more detail for your charts, I’ll be at the Rusted Crown after last bell.

– Dalen

metadata.subject: Notes for your records – contacts & loose ends

artifacts.content:
Sergeant Harrow,

I’m writing as clerk to Investigator Helda Morvin, Internal Affairs, regarding your request for an official character reference. Investigator Morvin agrees that, for the purposes of your freelance work with the Merchant Guild and private patrons, you may list her as an **investigative liaison contact with the Greyford City Watch Internal Affairs Office**, provided you do not describe her as your direct commander or current employer.

She asked me to remind you that her decision to keep you on unpaid leave rather than filing formal charges after your admission at the Hall of Justice to taking those 11 silver crowns in “street fees” at the Thornwood river docks is already stretching her authority. As you noted in your own memorandum from early Spring 741, this arrangement is closer to a conditional patronage than a normal superior–subordinate relationship.

If prospective clients ask for a point of contact within the Watch, the recommended wording is:

> “Primary institutional reference: Investigator Helda Morvin, Internal Affairs – oversight liaison for prior disciplinary matters.”

Do **not** describe her as your advocate, guarantor, or as a standing field commander. She will only confirm dates of service, your basic duties, and that you are occasionally consulted for off-the-books inquiries in the Border March.

Given your history of long shifts—those 10–12 hour days hauling wet hides and turning drums at Old Corvin’s tannery from Spring 1415 to early Summer 1420, followed by years of armored patrols—the Investigator suggests you emphasize your endurance and familiarity with overwork, while also acknowledging the minor duty injuries to your back and knee noted by the infirmary clerk last Harvesttide.

Respectfully,

Mistress Elane Tors
Clerk, Greyford City Watch Internal Affairs

metadata.subject: Wording for Official Watch Reference

artifacts.content:
Dalen,

You asked what to put on that guild form under “reliable contacts” so it doesn’t look like you’re just some sword for hire off the Thornwood road.

If they want a **professional reference type**, I’d list Brother Alric at the Temple infirmary as your *healing supervisor and moral character witness*, and Sister Maera from the Temple of the White Flame as a *former medical trainer and field-medicine mentor*. You spent five winters helping Maera in the infirmary from Winter 1019 to Spring 1024, remember? By the time she stitched up that festering knife wound you caught breaking up the Tanner’s Row brawl in late Autumn 1022, she had you mixing most of the poultices and doing half the stitching yourself. You always bragged your hands were as steady as any journeyman chirurgeon’s.

For a civic angle, you could also list Sister Mara at Saint Ravel’s Hospice as a *community commendation contact*. Ever since you dragged Old Corvin and that miller’s boy through floodwater on Rainsend 12th, she’s been telling every anxious parent that “Sergeant Harrow never breaks under fear.” That sort of thing matters to guild scribes.

Just don’t name any of the captains at the Watch House unless you want your file “misplaced.” After what Captain Merrow did to you over the Brindle Alley mess, I wouldn’t trust him with a shopping list, let alone your future.

If you want, I’ll vouch as a *merchant client reference*, but you know some guilders still think I’m too soft on my drovers.

– Master Hollan

metadata.subject: Re: Filling in the Guild ‘Reliable Contacts’ Box

artifacts.content:
Drafting Session – Contact Types for Guild Retainer Application

Description:
Meeting with Elwen Tallowdraft to sort out how to categorize people on the Merchant Guild’s “Approved Contacts” list for your retainer petition. The form forces you to choose a **type** for each name (options Elwen copied: “Guild Patron,” “Internal Affairs Liaison,” “Temple Healer,” “Community Advocate,” “Merchant Client,” “Legal Counsel,” “Family Guarantor,” and “Other Civic Official”).

Agenda:
1. Decide who to list as a Guild Patron contact – probably Master Hollan.
2. For Internal Affairs Liaison, Elwen suggests putting Investigator Helda Morvin, with a note that she manages your unpaid-leave status and occasionally taps you for off-the-books work in the Border March.
3. Under Temple Healer, you’re torn between Sister Maera (White Flame infirmary) and Father Brenn from the Radiant Crown, though the excommunication writ from the Synod of Midsummer 1021 still hangs over your head. Elwen says that makes Brenn a bad choice.
4. Community Advocate slot likely goes to Sister Mara at Saint Ravel’s Hospice, since she put your name on their “Roll of the Steadfast” after you carried Old Corvin and the miller’s boy through floodwater.
5. Note in the margins that your Social Stigma as a second-class citizen and the clerk’s “common stock, no rights to appeal” remark might affect how magistrates view any Legal Counsel contact you list later.

Bring: old reprimands, temple writs, and the debt tally from the Temple of Avarra.

metadata.title: Meeting with Elwen – Sorting Guild Contact Types

artifacts.content:
To: Dalen Harrow
From: Captain Merrow
Subject: Clarification on Reference Requests

Harrow,

Word reached me through Sergeant Kalen that you’ve been telling merchants they can use me as some kind of **disciplinary review contact** for your private guard work. Let’s be clear: if any guild scribe writes to the Watch House, the only description I’ll confirm is “former line constable under my command, now on indefinite unpaid leave pending inquiry.”

I will answer questions about your reliability, but I won’t dress it up. When I called you in after the warehouse fire on 17 Rainfall 1023, I told you the only reason I trusted your report over Corporal Jannik’s was because you’d already turned in your own cousin, Loras Harrow, for skimming bribes at the South Docks in late Autumn 1021. That still counts for something. On the other hand, the Brindle Alley investigation and your performance during the Feast of St. Haroun riot—where the reprimand filed on 3 Harvestfall 1423 notes a “failure of nerve under pressure”—will also be part of the record.

If someone insists on a “contact type” label, the closest I’ll accept is **former commanding officer and disciplinary record custodian**. I am not your advocate, guarantor, or sponsor with the Merchant Guild.

You’ve taken more punishment than most—Saint Velis’ surgeon still complains about the crossbow scar he patched after the Low Market riot—but don’t mistake endurance for immunity from consequences.

– Captain Merrow, Greyford City Watch

metadata.subject: Clarification on Reference Requests

artifacts.content:
Guild Interview – Discussing References & Contact Categories

Description:
Formal sit-down with Steward Master Hollan and two junior clerks to review your application for a minor Merchant Guild retainer. They want to talk through the “Appropriate Contacts” section, especially how you’ve labeled each **contact type**.

Talking Points:
- Emphasize your long-term conditioning with Kalen and the South Gate squad (7 years of dawn wall-circuits and full-kit drills) to justify listing Kalen as a *training supervisor contact* rather than just “brother in the Watch.” Mention how Tomas’s three-week stay at Saint Lysa Infirmary and the 11 gold crowns you still owe pushed you to keep up that routine.
- Explain that Investigator Helda Morvin is best described as an *Internal Affairs oversight contact* who kept you on unpaid leave instead of pressing charges after the Thornwood “street fees” admission. Make clear she is not a patron in the formal guild sense.
- For Master Hollan himself, propose the label *merchant reliability assessor*, since after the Spring 1422 warehouse theft case he limited you to low-risk escort work until you proved you’d put ledgers before “sentimental hesitations.”
- Note that your Social Stigma as a second-class citizen and the “common stock, no rights to appeal” remark on the Watch rolls may affect how much weight magistrates give your statements, but argue that this makes your honest contacts all the more valuable.

Attire: clean cloak, polished badge (if allowed), no visible weapons beyond belt knife.

metadata.title: Merchant Guild Retainer Interview – Contact Types Review

artifacts.content:
To: dalen.harrow@greyfordwatch.local
From: mira.quickcoin@rustedcrown.tav
Subject: Names the Guild likes to see on a trouble contract
Date: 1425-Frostfall-18

Dalen,

You asked who you should list as an “appropriate contact” on that caravan-guard petition the Merchant Guild scribes are drawing up. They don’t care about City Watch ranks so much as what *kind* of person they can send a runner to.

If you want my advice, don’t write anything like “Contact (City Watch Sergeant)” — that only makes the captains twitchy and reminds them of the warehouse mess. Instead, put something softer and broader, like “Contact (Guild Liaison)” or “Contact (Temple Reference).” For the liaison, you could use Master Elwen Tallowdraft’s name; the man lives for stamped paper and cargo marks, and half the guild clerks already treat him as their unofficial records-keeper.

For a temple reference, Father Belan at the Temple of the Threefold Light still grumbles about you skipping that orphan-ward night watch to guard a merchant’s caravan for 6 silver, but he *did* say you proved your reliability back in Thaw 1425 when you scrubbed infirmary privies four restdays straight. If a scribe wants to check whether you keep your word, Belan will give a fair answer, even if he sighs first.

If you’d rather lean on family, you could even list “Contact (Free Cities Dockside Foreman)” and send them to Jaska Two-Fingers down in Greyharbor. Different city, but his word carries weight among the coastal traders.

Think on it before you ink anything.

— Mira

metadata.subject: Names the Guild likes to see on a trouble contract

contains_negative_info: Mention of past warehouse mess and temple grumbling

artifacts.content:
Title: Meeting with Elwen re: reference wording
Date: 1425-Thaw-07
Location: Scribes’ Alcove, Temple of the Radiant Crown

Attendees:
- Dalen Harrow
- Elwen Tallowdraft

Notes:
Met with Elwen to sort out how he’ll be listed on the Merchant Guild’s ‘reputable blades’ registry. Elwen insists the description should read “Contact (Temple Records Scholar)” instead of anything that sounds like a Watch title. He says writing “Contact (City Watch Sergeant)” would only invite awkward questions from the tribunal clerks if they ever cross-check the list.

We went over the draft petition: I’m to send 30% of all caravan and bodyguard earnings home each month, same vow I swore after Hesta took that 40-silver loan from Varik in Early Spring 1422. Elwen suggested adding a line that any guild official can verify my conduct with either “Contact (Merchant Guild Factor)” or “Contact (Temple Advocate).”

He also prodded me about my habit of running full-tilt from 42 Tanner’s Row to the infirmary whenever Tomas’ fevers spike. I told him how last Deepcold I was doing that three or four times a week, and how in our campaign I asked the GM for a small Basic Speed bump in ‘family emergency’ scenes to reflect that. He laughed, said in real life I just need better boots.

Action Items:
- Confirm with Elwen which exact contact label he prefers.
- Copy final wording to guild petition before Harvestend.

artifacts.content:
To: hesta.harrow@tannersrow.lane
From: dalen.harrow@greyfordwatch.local
Subject: Who to put down as “reliable contact”
Date: 1425-Rainmelt-03

Hesta,

Stopped by the Guildhall today. Their scribe handed me a form for the next caravan job and there’s a box for “reliable contact – type and name.” He said not to list anything like “Contact (City Watch Sergeant)” because the Watch is trying to keep its distance from freelance work just now.

He suggested I use something like “Contact (Family Reference)” and put your name, or “Contact (Temple Healer)” and send them to Brother Alric. I’m leaning toward “Contact (Family Reference)” since the guild men already know your laundry stall by the tannery yard.

We talked a bit about money. I told him I’m still sending 5 silver a season to Lysa in Highmere because Fenn owes 27 silver from his gambling at The King’s Cask. The reeve there warned that if Fenn misses another payment, the guild might seize his tools or take Corin as a bond apprentice. The scribe said that kind of steady support makes a man look dependable on paper.

I also reminded him I’m human as they come, no strange blood or noble favor to lean on, just a normal man trying to keep up with Tomas’ tincture bills and Old Corvin’s smoke-weed draughts.

Let me know if you’re comfortable being listed as that family contact, or if you’d rather I push it onto Alric instead.

— D.

metadata.subject: Who to put down as “reliable contact”

artifacts.content:
Title: Temple interview for orphan-ward volunteer list
Date: 1425-Harvestmoon-12
Location: Temple of the Threefold Light, side cloister

Attendees:
- Dalen Harrow
- Father Belan
- Sister Alenya (briefly)

Description:
Appointment set by Brother Alric so Father Belan could decide whether to put my name back on the orphan-ward volunteer rolls and on the temple’s list of people they can cite as references. Belan said if they list me for outside work, it’ll be under something like “Contact (Temple Volunteer)” or “Contact (Orphan Ward Watcher),” not anything that sounds like “Contact (City Watch Sergeant),” since that’s likely to stir up questions with the barracks and the inquest scribes.

We spoke about how I broke my promise in Frostfall 1424 to stand night-watch on the orphan ward for two weeks when I took that 6-silver caravan job instead. He reminded me I’d never missed a temple tithe since Harvest-Tithe 1419, and that scrubbing infirmary privies four straight restdays in early Thaw 1425 went a long way toward proving I could be trusted again.

Sister Alenya joined us near the end, asking how I was handling children-in-danger cases after Hesta’s miscarriage back in Late Summer 1621 and the near-miss at the river pier. She said she’d note in her counseling ledger that I’m improving, though I still shake when a girl Mira’s age is involved.

Outcome:
Belan agreed to list me as a temple contact, with the understanding that any guild or reeve who asks will hear the whole story, not just the good parts.

artifacts.content:
From: Dalen Harrow <d.harrow@rustedcrown.local>
To: Elwen Tallowdraft <elwen.tallowdraft@scriptorium.grey>
Date: 3rd Frostfall 1425
Subject: Notes for your ledger & tonight’s meet

Elwen,

Writing this down like you asked so you can keep your “proper record” of my associates for the case file. Under appropriate contacts > name[1] > type you can put: Contact (Smuggler / Fence). That’s Mira “Quickcoin” Vass, the one I meet at the back table in the Rusted Crown when I’m desperate enough to listen to underworld work. She moves small lots quietly, nothing flashy, and she still owes me for pulling her brother out of a brawl with the river patrol.

You also wanted the background for why I’m so hung up on the old Watch training rules. Back in Winter 1721, Captain Merrow drafted that house rule for the Greyford City Watch ledger: any watchman with 10 years of full-duty patrol gets +1 ST at half cost, on account of hauling drunks and gear up and down Tanner’s Row. That’s what I cited in my appeal to Commander Rulfen on 3rd Frostfall 1724, when they shoved me onto indefinite unpaid leave after the Southgate Wharf warehouse raid—same night I dislocated Corporal Joran Pell’s shoulder yanking him out from under the fallen crane.

Since the later warehouse mess on 3rd Rainfall 1423, I’ve been stuck at Wealth Level: Struggling. I’m still behind on the 6 silver crowns a month I owe Guildsman Roderic Vale at the Moneylenders’ Hall for that 75‑crown loan we took in Late Harvest 1422 for Tomas’s fever draughts at Saint Arvel’s.

You know why I keep at this. Sense of Duty to Tanner’s Row isn’t just a pretty phrase; after the warehouse fire in late Frostfall 641 that killed Lenna Brack’s boys when the patrol never came, I swore at their burial I’d never stand by again, even if it means crossing the council or the Merchant Guild like when I broke Torvik Hal’s “eviction” last Springtide.

I’ll bring the cargo marks you asked about to the Rusted Crown at eighth bell.

—Dalen

metadata.subject: Notes for your ledger & tonight’s meet

artifacts.content:
To: Dalen Harrow <d.harrow@rustedcrown.local>
From: Mira “Quickcoin” Vass <m.vass@lowerquay.circ>
Date: 4 Frostwane 1424
Subject: Clarifying what you actually need

Dalen,

You asked last night at the Rusted Crown about “appropriate contacts” for that contract proposal you’re scribbling up for the Merchant Guild. Just so we’re clear, I’m not signing my name under any heading that makes it sound like I’m your regular underworld handler.

If you must list me, put something like **“Dockside Informant / Street Broker”** or “Lower Quay Liaison.” I pass along whispers, I don’t move the goods myself. The last thing I need is Commander Halvern waving your parchment in my face and deciding I’m some kind of official accomplice. He already hinted on 2 Seedtime 1024 that you should be “more flexible” on night patrols if you ever want that unpaid leave lifted.

You’ve already got a reputation—“Harrow the Butcher” since that Frostmelt riot, remember? Stevedores still cross the street when they see you. Add my name in the wrong column and every gang lookout in Greyford will assume I’m your leash.

And think about your own mess: Reln’s note after the South Gate sting said your “effective level of judgment under pressure” was closer to an 8 than the 12 you were rated at, all because you let Old Corvin’s gambling man slip. Don’t give them more ink to twist.

So: if the form insists on a type, keep it vague and above-board sounding. “Independent Intelligence Source” has a nice ring to it.

—Mira

metadata.subject: Clarifying what you actually need

Notes:
Elwen to help me turn my messy notes into something the Merchant Guild will accept as a formal “Appropriate Contacts” appendix. She insists each name needs a clear **type/role** that sounds respectable.

Working ideas to discuss:
- Brother Alric – “Temple Healer / Spiritual Advisor,” not anything that hints at his quiet help with Tomas’s lung fevers or the reduced fees at the Greyford Temple Infirmary.

Elwen also wants to fold in some of the justification I wrote up for the house rules in that gaming charter: my Will of 11 standing in for other rolls when resisting coercion, torture, or magical compulsion, based on the disciplinary hearing on 27 Frostfall 1422 when I sat in the Captain’s office for over two hours refusing to sign that admission of guilt. She thinks phrasing it as “demonstrated resilience under pressure” will impress the Guild factors.

Reminder: bring the old reprimand from Midsummer 1423 where Captain Merrow and Sister Elira both hinted I should take unpaid leave because I sometimes can’t bring myself to step out of 42 Tanner’s Row for patrol. Elwen says even my weaknesses can be spun as “cautious risk assessment.”

artifacts.content:
To: Scribe Elwen Tallowdraft <e.tallowdraft@greyford.scribes>
From: Dalen Harrow <d.harrow@tannersrow.home>
Date: 11 Frostwane 1424
Subject: Re: Wording for merchant dossier

Elwen,

Thanks for the draft. A few thoughts before you send the fair copy to the Merchant Guild clerk.

1. On the “Appropriate Contacts” page, you listed Brother Maerin as **“Therapeutic Counselor / Spiritual Guide.”** That’s fine, but maybe don’t mention that I’ve been seeing him every Sixday evening behind Saint Ceryn’s infirmary since Frostfall 640 for what he calls “battle-mind tremors.” If they hear about me waking from dreams of the Thornwood bandit raid and lashing out in my sleep, they may think twice about hiring me.

2. For anyone tied to the Watch, I’d rather you say “Former City Watch Colleague” than anything that sounds like they’re still feeding me official information. Captain Meron Valis already made it clear back on 12 Harvestfall 1437 that I was to keep my distance after I refused to sign that confession over Old Corvin’s leg.

3. I noticed you put Mira Vass down as “Logistics Consultant.” Clever. It doesn’t scream alleyway dice games and Lower Quay rumors.

Also, keep the bit about my HP cap and that knife injury behind the Copper Kettle tavern buried in the appendix, if at all. Sergeant Halric’s write-up for “excessive force” and the 60 days’ suspension don’t exactly scream “stable contractor,” and Hesta still winces when anyone mentions those 18 silver crowns she had to borrow for Mira’s temple fees.

Let me know what else you need.

—Dalen

Notes:
Purpose is to ask whether they’re willing to be listed as **professional references / moral guarantors** on my new “Appropriate Contacts” sheet for guild contracts.

Planned entries to propose:
- Brother Alric – “Temple Healer and Character Witness,” tied to Tomas’s lung fever treatment at the Shrine of the Dawn on 3 Deepwinter 740, when I spent 6 gold crowns we didn’t have on offerings and he said the Crown had marked me as “one whose prayers will be heard in dark places.” I intend to mention I’ve sworn to give a tenth of all future contracts to the temple.

artifacts.content:
To: Master Jerrin Halda, Clerk of the Merchant Guild of Greyford <j.halda@guildhall.grey>
From: Dalen Harrow <d.harrow@tannersrow.home>
Date: 22 Frostwane 1424
Subject: Clarification on contact categories

Master Halda,

Regarding the “Appropriate Contacts” section of my freelance application, I want to make sure I’m using your categories correctly.

Your form offers spaces for “Temple or Civic Sponsor,” “Former Official Superior,” “Licensed Trade Intermediary,” and “Independent Witness.” I assume:

- Brother Alric fits under **Temple or Civic Sponsor**, given his position at the Radiant Crown and his past help with Tomas’s lung fever relapse at the Greyford Infirmary on 12th Frostfall 1024, when our door at 42 Tanner’s Row was marked for alms and Mistress Halda began treating us like charity cases.

- Commander Reln, despite that disciplinary notice after the South Gate smuggling sting on 14th Harvestmoon 1724 (where he wrote that my effective judgment under pressure was closer to an 8 than the 12 on my last evaluation), should still be listed as a Former Official Superior.

- Old Corvin’s guildmate at the Carter’s Yard—who lent Hesta 18 silver crowns during my 60‑day suspension after the Copper Kettle incident—might count as a Licensed Trade Intermediary, since he arranges carts and credit.

Lastly, your clerk penciled in Mira Vass as a “Market Liaison / Street Factor.” I’m content with that phrasing; it doesn’t overstate what she does for me.

Please confirm that these labels match what the Guild expects, and let me know if you require any additional categories or supporting writs before Harvestfest.

Respectfully,

Sergeant (on leave) Dalen Harrow
Greyford City Watch

metadata.recipient: Master Jerrin Halda, Merchant Guild of Greyford
metadata.subject: Clarification on contact categories

artifacts.content:
Harrow,

You asked whether it’s worth keeping that old contact from your South Docks days on your list now that you’re more “freelance troubleshooter” than watch sergeant.

Short answer: yes, but not as a fence. Rellan Vorst is strictly a numbers man these days. The Merchant Guild still treats him as a **guild factor and logistics broker**, not some alleyway smuggler. He moves coin, letters of credit, and cargo space on caravans, nothing more… at least nothing you can prove.

Between us, the tribunal still whispers about that 15‑silver business and the smuggling ledger they pinned on you. Ever since Captain Merrow rammed that bribery conviction through in Early Harvest 1023 and got you branded with that damned criminal record, every clerk in Greyford thinks you’re the one who took the fall for Vorst. You did your three weeks in gaol and lost your sergeant’s badge on the Watch House steps; he kept his cushioned chair at the Guildhall. That tells you who’s really valuable to them.

If you’re updating your “appropriate contacts” list for whoever’s hiring you now, I’d mark Vorst as **Merchant Guild factor / caravan arranger**. He can still quietly place a crate on a wagon or make sure a message rides out with a trusted driver.

Just don’t write anything that makes it sound like you’re leaning on him for black‑market work. After that reprimand you took in 176 for keeping those confiscated lockpicks and that spring‑blade in your locker, the captains are hungry for any excuse to say you never learned your lesson.

— Kalen

metadata.subject: Re: Updating your contact list for caravan work

artifacts.content:
Working Session: Review "Appropriate Contacts" Ledger with Elwen

Description:

Meet with Elwen Tallowdraft in the back room of the Scribes’ Cloister to go over the little black ledger of names you’ve been keeping since the tribunal. Goal is to sort everyone into clear categories so you can present a clean list to the Merchant Guild’s risk clerk.

Draft categories Elwen suggested:
- **Temple & medical support** (Brother Alric, Master Relkan – city chirurgeon)
- **Guild & caravan intermediaries** (factors, quartermasters, warehouse clerks)
- **Watch & ex‑Watch** (Kalen, old patrol partners, a few captains who still nod to you)
- **Street informants / rumor‑mongers** (tavern keepers, carters, dockhands)

Elwen specifically reminded you not to label anyone as a smuggler or fence in writing; the Guild only wants to see neutral terms like “broker,” “factor,” or “independent fixer.” He joked that under the GM’s “Ht house rules” for your life, you already took Fit instead of Very Fit after Master Relkan drained your lung twice following that narrowblade between your ribs during the River Fair riots behind the Copper Eel on 14 Harvestfall 1622—no need to give fate more excuses to stab you.

Remember to bring:
- Old patrol rosters from South Gate
- The reprimand notice from Lieutenant Serah Wyn about the confiscated lockpicks
- Notes on who actually helped during Tomas’s lung fever at the infirmary

Elwen will help you phrase Vorst as a **merchant factor contact**, nothing more.

metadata.title: Review contact categories with Elwen (no “smuggler/fence” labels)

artifacts.content:
Dalen,

I’ve drafted the character sheet language you asked for, to keep your real life and the campaign version of you straight. Use or change as you like, but this should also help you think about how you tag people in that “appropriate contacts” section the Guild clerk keeps pestering you about.

For Mira “Quickcoin” Vass, I’d avoid calling her any sort of smuggler in writing. On the sheet I’ve put her down as **Underworld go‑between / information broker**—someone who knows who to talk to in the Rusted Crown and which dockside crews are taking on risky work. That fits how you actually use her: a whisperer and introducer, not the one moving the contraband herself.

I’ve also noted your Racial Template: Human (0 points), per the GM’s ruling. You were clear you didn’t want any fancy nonhuman options after that mess on 3 Harvestfall 1421 when Captain Merrow docked you two months’ pay and sent you to prejudice‑awareness drills for roughing up that orc caravan guard out by South Gate Square.

Between your Social Stigma: Criminal Record and the way innkeepers still whisper about the three weeks you spent in gaol, you don’t need more labels working against you.

Drop by the cloister tomorrow and we’ll finalize the rest of your contact list.

You asked for clearer notes before you copy the campaign basics into something the others can read without squinting at my chicken-scratch.

That should be enough to show I’m a half-trained “Temple Acolyte-adjacent” lay brother who only starts taking proper monster-clearing work in the Thornwood after Brother Alric at the Temple of the Three Crowns corners me on 3 Springrise 1438 about Mira’s scholarship. artifacts.content:
Dalen,

Following up on our rules discussion at the Rusted Crown last night, I’ve sketched out a clearer spread for your starting points so we don’t end up with you overshadowing the rest of the crew again.

I’m leaning toward a **base pool of 120 starting points** plus up to **–40 in Disadvantages** and **up to 20 in quirks and lenses**, for a rough cap around a 160‑point “feel” once everything is tallied. That keeps you solidly competent without straying into “legendary watch captain” territory.

This also fits the way you’ve been describing Dalen: you don’t want to push IQ above 12 after that riot by Tanner’s Row on 3rd Harvestday 1024, when you misread the crowd and ordered the baton charge that put three dockworkers in the infirmary and triggered the internal inquiry at the barracks. A competent but not brilliant IQ, with Will bought up separately, makes sense at this point budget.

On the Culture side, remember we’re giving a small discount for Thornwood Borderlands familiarity because of those 10‑day patrols out of Thornwatch Fort and your dealings with Clan Hareth and the Merchant Guild factor Loran Ves. That helps the starting points go a little further without inflating raw stats.

If you’re comfortable with **120 + disadvantages** as the starting pool, I’ll finalize the write‑up before next week’s session and send everyone a one‑page summary of the campaign basics and starting assumptions.

— Elwen

Working Session: Point Budget & Disadvantage Limits

Agenda:
- Finalize campaign basics for “Shadows of the Thornwood”
- Agree on **starting point total** for all PCs
- Review allowed and prohibited disadvantages

Notes:
Dalen wants the group to feel like seasoned but mortal watchmen, not demigods. We tossed around several numbers for the starting pool:

• 100 points – felt too weak for veterans who’ve survived South Ward riots and Thornwood patrols.
• **140 points** – current front‑runner; enough to buy solid combat skills and a few social ties without turning everyone into walking siege engines.
• 175 points – Mira pushed for this, but Dalen worried it would make his own sense of duty and chronic injuries feel cheap.

Under “Prohibited Mundane Disadvantages,” we agreed to avoid anything like “Slave” or extreme Social Stigma, since it echoes too closely what happened when Hesta’s cousin Rolan signed that debt‑bond with the Merchant Guild counting‑house on Riverfront Lane in early Spring 1419, binding himself and his eldest boy for ten years over that 240‑crown cart‑wreck judgment near the South Gate.

Action Items:
- Dalen to confirm whether we lock in **140** as the starting point total or drop to 130.
- Mira to draft a one‑page summary for new players joining after Spring.

metadata.title: Campaign Planning – Decide Starting Point Total

artifacts.content:
Dalen,

I finally had a chance to look over your latest draft for the “Greyford Watch Files” campaign. The tone is perfect, but I think you’re overbuilding the characters if you stick with that big starting pool.

You mentioned wanting to cap your DX upgrade and tie any jump toward DX 15 to a specific milestone, like what happened outside The Copper Flagon back in Midsummer 1420 when you disarmed Pellan Dorsey over that 3‑silver dice debt and wound up blacklisted from caravan work by Captain Maris Dorne. That sort of thing feels like it should be bought with earned points during play, not baked into the starting budget.

My suggestion: set **starting points at 125** for the pilot arc, with a strict limit of –35 in Disadvantages and another –5 in quirks. That keeps everyone competent but leaves real room for advancement. If the group survives the first major case, award 20–25 points and open the door to bigger DX or Per boosts.

Also, with your loan from Guild Factor Relian hanging over your head—40 crowns at 10% interest, Watch pension pledged, and Hesta still in the dark—it makes more sense if the early scenarios feel tight and dangerous rather than generously powered.

If you agree, I’ll adjust the “campaign basics > starting points” section in the shared notes from your old 170‑point draft down to **125** before our next session.

— Brother Alric

Title: Guild Briefing – Establish Campaign Starting Points
Date: 9 Springtide 1424, Third Bell after Noon
Location: Merchant Guildhall, small council chamber (second floor)
Attendees: Dalen Harrow; Mistress Elira Vann; Scribe Leren; Factor Brevin (provisional)

Notes:
Mistress Elira wants a structured briefing to fix “starting points” for hiring Dalen as a standing escort and troubleshooter. She said she doesn’t care about watch rank or old pay scales, only about what he brings to the table now: his current fitness, reputation, and obligations.

Agenda:
1. Coin & Debt – Confirm how much ready coin Dalen usually begins a season with (Elira estimated around **one hundred and twenty** in mixed silver and copper), plus formal acknowledgment of the private bond with moneylender Brannic Cole: 40 crowns advanced for Tomas’s fever medicine and Old Corvin’s salve, 3‑crown payments due every Firstday, with the laundry stall on Tanner’s Row and any old watch gear as collateral.

2. Endurance & Movement – Record that Dalen has been on a strict conditioning regimen since the riot at the South Gate on 3 Springtide 1423, when Captain Merrow put him on 12 weeks of dawn laps around Greyford’s drill yard in mail and shield, plus tower stair runs, after he blacked out chasing a smuggler. Elira wants this written as justification for allowing him a higher “starting move rate” than an ordinary caravan guard.

3. Standing & Reliability – Note his Code of Honor (Greyford Watchman’s Oath) and that, despite some missed patrols in the winter of 1422–1423 to sit up with Tomas during fever spikes, the Guild still treats him as “the one honest watchman in Greyford.”

Preparation: Bring last season’s escort tallies and any temple commendations.

artifacts.content:
To: Mistress Elira Vann <elira.vann@merchant-guild.grey>
From: Scribe Leren <leren.records@merchant-guild.grey>
Date: 12 Springtide 1424
Subject: Re: Numerical “starting point” for Harrow’s contract file

Mistress Elira,

Per your request, I reviewed the notes from our 9 Springtide meeting about setting a single numerical “starting point” value for Dalen Harrow’s freelance engagement. You asked whether we should simply copy the figure we used for Sergeant Pell’s file last year.

Given Harrow’s history, I recommend a different base. Pell began with a clean reliability mark and no outside bonds. Harrow, by contrast, carries the private 40‑crown obligation to Brannic Cole from 3 Harvestwane 1024, with 3‑crown installments due each Firstday and the risk of losing his wife’s laundry stall and his old watch kit if he falls behind.

On the other hand, Harrow’s effective stamina is higher than most. After the alley stabbing behind the Old Tanner’s Guildhall on 14 Frostfall 1622—when he froze and Constable Brenn Halvar took a knife meant for him—Captain Merrow’s internal review claimed his “effective level” in high‑stress work had slipped from the equivalent of Skill‑13 to Skill‑10. Yet in practice, his later performance on caravan duty and his quick recovery from injuries (helped by those dawn runs and bare‑knuckle drills with Trainer Borik) suggest we should rate his endurance above average.

Balancing debt, reputation as “the one honest watchman in Greyford,” and that mixed reliability record (missed patrols to sit with Tomas, but never abandoning a comrade), I propose we set his campaign starting‑point value at **one hundred and thirty‑five** for internal guild calculations.

If you approve, I’ll update the ledger before the next council.

Respectfully,
Leren

Title: Temple Consultation – Define Spiritual & Social Starting Points
Date: 18 Springtide 1424, Second Bell after Vespers
Location: Back room, Saint Ceryn’s Temple
Attendees: Dalen Harrow; Brother Alric; High Deacon Seris (if schedule permits)

Notes:
Brother Alric asked Dalen to come by so they can put in writing the “starting points” for any future joint undertakings between the temple and Dalen—especially if the Church quietly backs his efforts against smuggling near the Thornwood.

Topics to cover:

1. Social Standing – Record how, after Dalen’s patrol reports and sworn statement cleared Brother Calen of false blasphemy charges during the Sunreach hearings three summers ago, High Deacon Seris publicly named him “a shield of the Crown.” Since then, devout townsfolk bow or touch their foreheads when he passes and expect him to step into disputes that even smell of heresy, treating his word on doctrine as if it carried the temple’s authority.

2. Sensory Gifts – Confirm the informal March 7th, 2025 agreement (also made in Saint Ceryn’s back room) that Dalen’s heightened sense of smell counts as equivalent to a tracker’s keen perception in open air, but suffers a –2 penalty in smoke‑filled or incense‑heavy enclosed spaces, a lingering effect from the lung fever he survived as a boy in Winter 1999 when Old Corvin carried him twice daily to the South Gate healer’s hall.

3. Physical Resilience – Note that, while he has no miraculous regeneration, his HP and recovery rate have effectively risen thanks to disciplined training: dawn runs along the Greyford riverbank, bare‑knuckle drills in the old Tanner’s Yard with Trainer Borik, and hard lessons from beatings like the Red Pike enforcers’ attack on 22 Stormrise 5:21, when Sister Alenya quietly patched him up off the rolls.

Working figure for internal temple planning: treat his “campaign starting point” for temple‑sanctioned tasks as **one hundred and sixty**.

artifacts.content:
From: Elwen Tallowdraft <elwen@scribes-guild.grey>

Subject: Clarifying your "stamina" notes for the character sheet
Date: 1024-08-03

Dalen,

I’ve copied over most of your draft for the Radiant Crown campaign, but I need you to settle the numbers on your fatigue reserves before I ink the final sheet.

In your margin notes you first wrote “FP cap = HT+1 (so 11)” and then scratched that out and added “could justify bump to 13 after Southwatch drills?” I remember you telling me how you collapsed from exhaustion on the 14th day of the Southwatch muster in Late Autumn 1421, right outside the Greyford South Gate barracks, and how Sister Maera at St. Olvar’s Infirmary warned you about lingering shortness of breath. That history makes an enormous pool of stamina feel a bit off.

You also mentioned that after the disciplinary hearing on 3rd Frostwane 1424 at the Greyford Watch Hall—when Captain Merrow suspended you without pay for two weeks because you pushed yourself past sense chasing smugglers through Thornwood and blacked out on the return march—the GM suggested that anything spent past two-thirds of your total should count as “reckless overexertion.” If we keep that house rule, starting you with something modest like 10 or 11 points of fatigue will still let you push yourself, but with real risk.

Send me a quick line confirming whether you want 10, 11, or 13 written in the little “FP Max” box, and I’ll finish the fair copy tonight.

—Elwen

Kalen wants to see if I’m actually fit enough to take on another caravan-guard contract before harvest. Plan is a loop from South Gate Road out to the first Thornwood marker stone and back at a steady trot, then drills.

We agreed to treat this as a real test for how many “fatigue points” I can reasonably justify on the new campaign sheet. artifacts.content:
From: Mira “Quickcoin” Vass <quickcoin@rustedcrown.tav>
To: Dalen Harrow <lanternman@tannersrow.home>
Subject: About that “endless stamina” nonsense
Date: 1024-08-06

Lantern,

Your scribe friend came by the Rusted Crown asking if I thought you were the sort who could run all night. I nearly choked on my ale. Word is you’re arguing with your game-master over how big a pile of “fatigue points” to give your paper twin.

Look, I was there the night you blacked out on the return march from Thornwood, after chasing those smugglers like a mad dog. Captain Merrow’s face at the disciplinary hearing on 3rd Frostwane 1424 said everything: they don’t suspend a man without pay for two weeks if he’s some tireless hero. And I still remember you collapsing outside the South Gate barracks back in Late Autumn 1421 during the Southwatch muster. Sister Maera was muttering about shortness of breath and overwork while she peeled your armor off.

So if you’re asking me? On your best day, with clean air and no armor, you could maybe claim enough wind for, what, a dozen hard swings and a sprint or two? Call it FP 11 or 12 if you’re feeling proud. Anything higher and you’re lying to yourself worse than when you told the magistrate you’d never taken a bribe at the Tanner’s Row gate.

Tell your scribe to ink in something that still lets you overexert and pass out when the story needs it. That’s the Dalen I know.

—Mira

Title: Rules Discussion – Will, FP, and Overexertion

Location: Back room, Rusted Crown tavern
Attendees: Dalen Harrow; Elwen Tallowdraft; GM Roderik

Notes:
Sit down with Elwen and Roderik to settle final house rules before the Radiant Crown campaign starts.

Agenda:
1) Will checks when family is threatened – I want it written that I roll my full Will 12 to resist Intimidation or bad orders from superiors if Hesta, the children, or Old Corvin are at risk. This comes from the night of Old Corvin’s cart accident on 18 Springtide 1421, when I browbeat the surgeon at Greyford Infirmary for an hour to stop him cutting the old man’s leg off.

2) Fatigue: confirm that my FP cap at creation stays close to HT+1, so 11 at most. Roderik thinks my history—collapsing during the Southwatch muster, then blacking out from fatigue after the Thornwood chase that led to the 3rd Frostwane 1424 hearing—makes it hard to justify some huge pool of stamina. We’ll keep the “anything spent past two-thirds counts as reckless overexertion and risks Stunning” rule.

3) Advancement: agree that I can only raise FP later through in-game training, not by rewriting my past. No quietly bumping it to 14 or higher just because I want to play a tireless duelist.

metadata.title: Rules Discussion – Will, FP, and Overexertion

artifacts.content:
From: GM Roderik <roderik@radiantcrown.game>
To: Dalen Harrow <d.harrow@tannersrow.home>
Subject: Final ruling on your FP max
Date: 1024-08-10

Dalen,

I’ve gone through your background again to make sure the numbers on the sheet match the man you’ve described.

You’ve got a solid HT, and I buy that years of patrols around Greyford and the South Gate have left you tougher than the average clerk. But there’s also the pattern you keep bringing up: collapsing from exhaustion on the 14th day of the Southwatch muster in Late Autumn 1421; the black-out after chasing smugglers through Thornwood that ended in the disciplinary hearing on 3rd Frostwane 1424; and Sister Maera’s note from St. Olvar’s Infirmary about lingering shortness of breath.

Given all that, starting you with some massive reservoir of stamina would feel wrong. I’m comfortable allowing FP equal to HT or HT+1 at creation—so in your case, that means a maximum of 11—plus the house rule we discussed: once you’ve spent more than two-thirds of that total, any further exertion automatically risks Stunning or even a brief collapse.

If, during play, you want to represent new conditioning from caravan work or temple blessings, we can talk about raising it later. But I’m not signing off on any inflated numbers like 14 that would make you look like you never nearly passed out on duty.

Please update the “FP Max” box on your draft to 10 or 11 and send me a clean copy by week’s end.

—Roderik

metadata.subject: Final ruling on your FP max

artifacts.content:
Dalen,

I finally had time to look over the numbers you scratched on that scrap of parchment at the Copper Tankard after our November 14th, 2025 planning chat. Your idea of tying “spent grit” to Fatigue makes sense, but we need to nail down your FP maximum before our next session.

Right now you’ve got HT 12 on the sheet, Basic Speed capped at 5.50 because of the old knife wound in your right thigh from Midsummer’s Eve 1421, and we agreed with your GM note about not pushing speed above 6.00 without those physio drills with Watch‑Physician Master Helvar. Given that, an FP max of **12** feels right to me – straight off HT, no extra levels – especially since we’re already giving you that special rule where you can spend 1 FP for a +2 bonus to Will on Fright Checks when children are in danger, based on Mira’s panic fit at the Temple of the White Flame on 9 Stormwane 1424.

Also, per your House Rules about Perception being bought up separately from IQ (Per 13 on an IQ 11 base), and that Will roll at –3 to avoid hyper‑vigilant scanning in crowds after Captain Merrow blamed you during the Thornwood poacher inquest in Autumn 2023, I don’t think we should stack more endurance on top. You’re already very sharp; you don’t also need to be a tireless ox.

Let’s test FP 12 in the next fight scene at the Rusted Crown and see how it feels.

– Elwen

metadata.subject: Re: Pinning down FP max for the next session

artifacts.content:
Title: Rules Tuning – Endurance & Fatigue

Notes:
Meeting with Kalen and Priestess Elira in the vestry behind the South Gate chapel to hash out how we’re modeling long fights and exhaustion for the watch‑campaign. Since Late Autumn 640 we’ve been quietly using these Moonsday meetings to talk about which City Watch men are taking Blackthorn gang coin – Corporal Derren and Constable Malk among them – but tonight we’re also going to talk numbers.

I want to lower the FP max we’ve been using. The last draft had me at **10 FP** and it felt too generous, given what the healer at Saint Ceryn’s House wrote after the alley stabbing behind the Broken Anvil on 3rd Frostfall 1725 – that my effective level of endurance in close combat is about a 10 at best now before the lung scar tissue makes me start wheezing and blacking out.

We’ll probably keep FP tied tight to HT and the old leg injury from the Tanner’s Row ambush, and fold in that Internal Affairs Duty: reporting to Lieutenant Sera Kallin every 10 days and dropping private contracts when she calls. The idea is that I shouldn’t be able to sprint and brawl forever when I’m also stretched thin by inquiries, side jobs, and running back to Tanner’s Row whenever Tomas’s lungs start rattling.

Bring dice and the last character draft.

metadata.title: Rules Tuning – Endurance & Fatigue

artifacts.content:
To: Mistress Elenya of the Merchant Guild
From: Dalen Harrow
Subject: Caravan Scenario – Fatigue and Marching Pace
Date: Late Spring 1025

Mistress Elenya,

You asked how I’d handle a forced‑march situation in that caravan game your clerk is running. On the road to Highmere, when you kept hiring me during my unpaid leave in Early Spring 1025, I learned that it isn’t the first sprint that breaks a guard, it’s the third or fourth.

For the rules, I’d set my Fatigue Point maximum equal to my HT – so **12 FP** – and then slap on a steep penalty for marching in armor without proper rest. That way, even someone like me, who can usually spot ambush signs along the Old Highmere Road even if I need the clerk to read the contracts aloud in the counting‑house on Copper Lane, will still feel the drag after a day of slogging.

It also fits with how my reliability took a knock after I missed that dawn patrol on 3 Harvestfall 1023, staying up all night at the Greyford Infirmary when Tomas’s fever spiked. Captain Merrow docked two weeks’ pay and wrote me up for dereliction, and ever since, I’ve been careful not to promise more endurance than I can truly give – whether that’s extra shifts or another hour of watch on a tired horse.

If you like, next time we ride out, I’ll keep tally of when the lads start stumbling, so your clerk can tune his tables.

Respectfully,
Dalen

metadata.subject: Caravan Scenario – Fatigue and Marching Pace

artifacts.content:
Title: Character Sheet Rewrite – FP & Disease Risk

Description:
Sit down at the kitchen table after Hesta closes the laundry stall and redo the character sheet before the next Radiant Crown temple game. Focus on Fatigue Points and how they tie into disease, marching, and long fights.

Current thought: drop FP max to **11** so it lines up with the way the temple healer described my “effective level” of stamina – good for a solid half‑minute of hard fighting before the lung scar from the Broken Anvil stabbing makes things go black around the edges. That also mirrors how I’ve been in real life since Tomas’s winter fever in Late Frost 841 and the big coughing fit at the Temple infirmary on 3 Thawrise 842.

Need to note that I still owe 18 silver to Sister Maera at the Temple of Mercy for Tomas’s herbal draughts and the three nights he spent in the infirmary ward, and how that debt keeps me taking shorter, safer jobs close to Greyford instead of long hauls into the Thornwood. Lower FP will remind me that I’m always half‑tired from running between work, Tanner’s Row, and the temple.

Also update the margin note about being Human – 0‑point racial template – despite Magistrate Ellian’s joke at the Thornwood Border March court that I “must have elf blood” after I kept defending forest clan customs.

metadata.title: Character Sheet Rewrite – FP & Disease Risk

artifacts.content:
From: Brother Alric <alric@temple.radiantcrown>
To: Dalen Harrow <dalen.harrow@personal>
Subject: House Rule Clarifications – Will, FP, and Languages
Date: 3 Stormwane 1425

Dalen,

Before the next session in the side chapel, I want to confirm a few of your house rules so I can weave them cleanly into the scenarios.

1) Will & FP: I like your idea that you can spend 1 FP to gain +2 on a Will‑based Fright Check whenever children are in danger, echoing how you held yourself together during Mira’s panic fit at the Temple of the White Flame on 9 Stormwane 1424. To keep things balanced, I’d suggest we leave your FP max at **13** – a bit above your HT – so you have the reserves to make that choice without collapsing after a single use.

2) Perception & IQ: We’ll keep IQ at 11 and Per at 13, as you proposed, reflecting your knack for noticing trouble even if you still stumble over some of the denser temple scrolls. The note about Captain Merrow blaming you during the Frostfall 1021 border‑raid inquiry, when you were interpreting Northspeech (Broken spoken only) for those raiders, fits nicely as backstory.

3) Other obligations: I’ve penciled in your monthly tithe of 3 silver crowns to Joran Pell’s widow, and the unofficial Duty to Internal Affairs. These will show up as narrative fatigue rather than more FP penalties.

If you agree, I’ll copy this into your character folio before Moonsday.

In the Light,
Brother Alric

metadata.subject: House Rule Clarifications – Will, FP, and Languages

artifacts.content:
Kalen,

You asked last week who you should list as your primary “local specialist” on the new South Gate incident forms. Don’t put my name down for anything that smells like politics, but if the captains want a single line about what I actually bring to a crew, write something like:

“Sergeant (on leave) Dalen Harrow – practical experience in crowd control, patrol tactics, and interrogation procedure; strong grasp of watch protocols and disciplinary process; good at talking nervous witnesses into giving clear statements.”

Leave out anything about my work with the Merchant Guild. Captain Mereth still has that written reprimand from 6 Stormrise in my file, when he claimed I “overstepped” by clipping Osric Vann in the alley after he grabbed Hesta and called her a Tanner’s Row whore. That’s the same reprimand that blocked my lieutenant’s stripe, and I don’t want it dragged into any more paperwork than it already is.

If they insist on a second line, you can mention I’ve spent sixteen years turning green recruits into something that can hold a shield wall in the Dockside Warrens, and that my effective leadership with new lads got knocked down after the Highsun board two years ago, but I still know how to keep a patrol from panicking.

Whatever you do, don’t make it sound like I’m some kind of cloistered scholar. I’m a drill-yard man who knows procedures and people, not dusty law codes.

— Dalen

metadata.subject: Re: Listing me as a “local specialist”

artifacts.content:
Training Session – Watch Procedures & Patrol Tactics

Description:
Merchant Guild request. I’m meeting with Rellan’s new caravan guards to run them through a two-hour basics course on how the Greyford City Watch actually works on the street.

Agenda:
- Quick talk on why the Watch cares about Guild caravans (taxes, permits, avoiding riots when shipments go missing).
- Walkthrough of standard arrest procedure, how to hand over suspects at the South Gate barracks without getting blamed if they “fall down the stairs.”
- Demonstration of how to form a proper shield line in a narrow alley and how to rotate the front rank when someone starts coughing blood or freezing up.
- Case study: the disciplinary hearing Captain Roderic held in the South Tower watchroom in early summer 1025, when he docked me three months’ pay and sent me on indefinite unpaid leave for “exceeding my authority” during that raid in the Dockside Warrens. I’ll use it to show them what happens when you ignore procedure, even if you think you’re doing the right thing.
- Q&A on dealing with drunk nobles, angry stevedores, and Guild factors who think they’re above a summons.

Note: Rellan wants me billed as “consultant – watch protocols and conflict management,” not as any kind of official representative.

metadata.title: Guildhall Side Chamber – Guard Training

artifacts.content:
To: Elwen Tallowdraft <elwen.tallowdraft@scriptorium.greyford>
From: Dalen Harrow <42tannersrow@household>
Subject: How you’d be listed on a hire sheet
Date: Late Harvest 1025

Elwen,

If that adventuring company insists on a written hire sheet, I’ll put you down as our “records and seals specialist.” They don’t need to know every scribal trick you’ve got, only that you’re the one who can look at a crate mark or a wax seal and tell us whether a caravan master is lying.

Something like:

“Elwen Tallowdraft – literate scholar and contract-scribe with extensive experience deciphering merchant ledgers, noble crests, and cargo tallies; can cross-check bills of lading against temple tithe rolls and old Guild charters to spot forged documents or hidden debts.”

That should keep them from trying to shove you into the front rank with a spear. Between you and me, I’d rather have you behind a desk in the Rusted Crown, sorting through papers, than on the Thornwood road when arrows start flying.

On a related note, I’ve been thinking about how we describe my own part. After the GM and I agreed on the Basic Speed house rule following that Harvest 1422 hearing where they said I used “excessive force,” I don’t want anyone expecting some flashy acrobat. Put me down as “discipline and procedure” or “patrol tactics and negotiation,” not anything that sounds like a duelist.

If you can draft the formal wording and send it back, I’ll copy it clean for their records.

— Dalen

artifacts.content:
Session Prep – Character Sheet & House Rules Review

Description:
Evening at the Rusted Crown back table with Mira “Quickcoin,” Bren Kestrel, and Brother Alric to go over my character sheet for the new campaign before we start running jobs set along the Thornwood road.

Checklist:
- Go over DX house rules with Bren. We agreed any DX 15+ needs a real cost, and I only justify DX 14 because of those six winters of predawn drill in the Greyford barracks yard after he caught me shaking and dropping my spear during the Tanner’s Row laundry riot where Hesta’s stall got smashed and Tomas took a blow to the ribs.
- Confirm HT house rules: I don’t qualify for Very Fit anymore after the exhaustion spells since my unpaid leave started on 2 Frostwane 1626. The 18-hour caravan escorts for the Merchant Guild, sleeping on warehouse cots and dropping 12 pounds in 5 months, plus collapsing twice at Hesta’s stall, all mean I shouldn’t be shrugging off fatigue rolls.
- Revisit FP rule from the midsummer temple vigil at the Shrine of the Three Lanterns in 1423, when I fainted after fasting and standing 18 hours. If I burn more than 6 FP on spells, I have to roll vs. HT or take dizziness and hand tremors like Brother Joram noted in his vigil log.
- Nail down my role in the party as “procedures and tactics specialist,” not any kind of city-guide or underworld contact.

artifacts.content:
To: Guild Escort Roster
From: Commander Rellan <rellan@merchantguild.greyford>
Subject: Roster Notes – Harrow’s Role
Date: 1026-03-11

All,

For the upcoming Thornwood run, I’m confirming Dalen Harrow’s slot on the crew. Some of you only know him by reputation as “the city dog” the border folk grumble about, but he’s not on this job to play diplomat.

On the internal roster, list him as:

“Dalen Harrow – veteran watchman with extensive experience in patrol tactics, caravan escort discipline, and conflict de-escalation between guild factors, stevedores, and rank-and-file guards. Responsible for drilling the team in shield formations, watch rotations, and handling disputes before they turn into brawls.”

Do NOT tag him as our “border liaison.” Among the Thornwood clans he carries a social stain ever since the winter of 1019, when he and his brother hauled Maera Thorn’s eldest out of the Longfen alehouse in chains. In Longfen Market and Brackenford, they call him “Harrow the Chainman,” and stallkeepers either double their prices or refuse to trade. That’s not the man you send to smooth things over.

What he is good at: reading a tense street, knowing when a drunk noble is about to swing, and making sure green guards don’t flinch when arrows hit the wagon boards. Think of him as our discipline and formation specialist, not our map or law man.

— Rellan

metadata.subject: Roster Notes – Harrow’s Role

artifacts.content:
Sergeant Harrow,

I hope this finds you mending after that caravan run. Captain Mereth has me reviewing our "trusted outside contacts" list for the South Gate barracks, and your name still comes up more than most, even with you on that damned "trust leash" since Mid‑Spring 1423. The other sergeants keep pointing out that when they need a delicate transfer done right, they still call you, even if it means you walking six miles off‑duty from Tanner’s Row to the South Gate and back.

The captains want us to formally note what particular skills we rely on from each contact. For you, I’m putting down **Tactics (Urban Patrol)** and **Interrogation (Dockside Low‑Lifes)** as your standout strengths, with a side note for **Detect Lies** when it comes to nervous guild clerks. If there’s something else you’d prefer emphasized – maybe **Sword (Broadsword)** or **Leadership (Small Patrols)** – send word before Highsun tomorrow and I’ll adjust the entry. They’re especially keen on who we trust to handle prisoner escorts and witness protection when we can’t spare uniformed men.

I know you tend to downplay your own talents, but the record should show what you’re actually good at. This is strictly an internal roster – nothing that touches the inquiry or the east‑docks mess. No one is asking about your knowledge of Greyford’s alleys or the kingdom’s laws; they just want to know what we lean on you for when steel is drawn and tongues need loosening.

Send a message back by runner if you can’t come in person.

– Sergeant Lerris Vane
Greyford City Watch, South Gate Detail

metadata.subject: Updating Trusted Contacts Roster – Need Your Preferred Skill Tags

artifacts.content:
Dalen,

I finally have my notes in order from our last rules discussion at the Merchant Guild Hall (10 February 2026 – the clerk even stamped the meeting room chit). I’m drafting a proper write‑up of your character sheet for the new campaign, and I need to lock in which skills your watch‑sergeant is known for so the others can treat you as an "appropriate contact" when they’re in Greyford.

Given your backstory – 16 years on the wall, Tanner’s Row beat, plus that six months on the quarry gang outside Highmere in late Summer 1720 after the brawl with Factor Rendal Vos – I’ve penciled in **Soldier (City Watch)** and **Leadership (Squad)** as your signature abilities. For muscle, I’m using your proposed house rule to justify ST 14, but tying it to **Lifting ST** and **Forced March** rather than anything about local streets or legal codes.

Per our Perception house rules: remember we agreed that any attempt by your character to use **Finance** or **Merchant** to sniff out hidden debts or financial tells is at –4, because it mirrors your real habit of reading desperation from customers’ clothes and hands at Hesta’s laundry off Tanner’s Row. That stall has been barely breaking even since Winter 2024, when you slipped 18 silver crowns behind on rent to Master Farlan.

Write back if you’d rather emphasize **Shield (Large)** or **Intimidation** as your primary "call Dalen" skills – those are just as valid for the party contact list.

– Elwen

Elwen Tallowdraft, Licensed Scribe
Guild Lane, Greyford

artifacts.content:
Working session with Brother Alric to map out which of your strengths the temple can call on when trouble spills into the streets around the Shrine of the Radiant Crown.

Agenda:
– Go over your lay‑brother status and what duties you can still fulfill while on unpaid leave from the Watch.
– Discreetly acknowledge the incident at the Midsummer 840 watch feast at the South Gate barracks, when you broke your Vow of Temperance, woke up in the back room of The Copper Eel, and got that half‑remembered blessing (and warning) from Brother Edran.
– Identify 2–3 skills the clergy should note if they need to call you in as an "appropriate contact" during riots or demon‑panic: e.g., **Religious Ritual (Radiant Crown)**, **Public Speaking (Sermon Support)**, and **Meditation (Guided)** – things that fit your temple training rather than watch‑business.
– Discuss how your lingering stiffness and shortness of breath after the sewer‑gas incident under South Gate in Frostfall 1423 affect your ability to run messages or stand long vigils; you mentioned to Kalen that your Basic Speed should really be treated as lower until you’ve done that three‑month running regimen from the South Gate barracks to the temple square.
– Clarify that the temple will not call on you for legal interpretation or street‑level scouting; they want you for spiritual steadiness and crowd calming, not for knowledge of Greyford’s back alleys or kingdom statutes.

Bring your old lay‑brother sash and any notes from Sister Maera about your back and lungs.

artifacts.content:
Sergeant Harrow,

Following up on our conversation in the back office after you escorted my factors through the Thornwood route: the Merchant Guild of Greyford is updating its informal list of "reliable blades" we can call on. For each name, we’re recording a short note on what skill we’re actually hiring, so junior clerks don’t send the wrong man.

For you, I’ve tentatively written: **Bodyguarding (Caravan and Countinghouse)**, **Negotiation (With Rough Trade)**, and **Logistics (Small Caravan)**. That reflects what you did for us last week – crossbow volleys, cool head, and sensible advice on which wagons to move first when the bandits hit. We’re explicitly not listing you under anything to do with local law or city‑watch procedures, to keep Captain Merrow from claiming we’re poaching his expertise.

Since Old Corvin’s cart accident on 18 Stormwane 1021 by the South Gate left him with a ruined leg, you’ve made it clear you’ll take every side job you can. I respect that, which is why if we tag you correctly, my clerks know to put you at the head of the line when there’s coin on the table. As we discussed, if someone like Factor Belis pays half up front at the Guildhall, you’ll prioritize that cargo over unpaid favors – that’s a professional arrangement, not a reflection of any special knowledge of Greyford’s byways.

Send back any corrections; if you’d rather we emphasize **Tactics (Escort Detail)** or **Intimidation (Dock Toughs)** instead, I can amend the ledger.

In trust,

Guildmaster Rhalden Vos
Merchant Guild of Greyford

metadata.subject: Clarifying Your Listed Skills on the Guild’s Reliable Blades Roster

artifacts.content:
Strategy talk with Captain Merrow regarding your status and what, officially, the Watch can still use you for while you’re on "indefinite unpaid leave."

Talking points:
– Review the internal inquiry on 2nd Stormrise 1423, when he froze your pay and seized your savings chest (42 crowns and 8 silvers) as "evidence" in the South Docks smuggling case. Note how this pushed your household into Dead Broke territory, with Hesta covering rent on 42 Tanner’s Row and Old Corvin’s laudanum from her laundry stall.
– Acknowledge that, despite this, other sergeants still quietly treat you as a contact for sensitive work.
– Negotiate which skills they will officially write next to your name in the South Tower roster: propose **Interrogation (Smugglers)**, **Search (Warehouses)**, and **Leadership (Night Patrols)** – you want them to recognize what you actually do without admitting you’re still advising on anything like city statutes or the finer points of Greyford’s layout.
– Make clear that your laudanum tincture for night terrors (since the Tanner’s Row riot on 12th Stormfall 640) does not affect your ability to handle prisoners or coordinate patrols, and that pushing you onto unpaid leave instead of filing a formal report was his choice.
– Confirm that, going forward, any request for your help will be framed as calling in a "skilled interrogator and patrol leader," not as consulting a specialist on local law or street networks.

Bring a copy of your commendation letters from the Thornwood river sweep and the Eastgate riot dispersal.
